summary: Building a modern, elegant DJ website for DJ Fizz. Color palette: black base with electric green accents and glow effects (matching the DJ Fizz logo). Full-width cinematic layout with smooth scrolling, fade-in animations, and stage lighting effects. Sections include: hero, about (with portrait photo), services (4-column), premium add-ons, testimonials, event gallery, SEO section, 'More Than Just Music' section, and a booking form. Booking form submissions stored in backend with an admin page for viewing. No flat prices — custom packages messaging. Targeting weddings, corporate events, school dances, and private parties in Raleigh, NC.
architectural_notes: Color palette: black base with electric green accents and glow effects, matched to the DJ Fizz logo (no gold)
Booking form submissions stored in backend database; viewable via private admin page
No flat pricing displayed — use 'Custom Packages Built Around Your Vision' messaging
known_issues: Gallery section content wrapper still visible after attempted removal — needs to be fully deleted from Gallery.tsx
Booking form submissions are intermittently failing (second submission returned failure message); admin page not displaying stored submissions
Admin page at /admin is again blocking access with authentication gate in Version 15 — authentication check needs to be fully removed

existing_features.title: Update the entire site color palette to match the DJ Fizz logo (IMG_0756.png): deep black (#000000 / #0a0a0a) as the base, electric neon green (#39ff14 or closest match to logo green) as the primary accent color for buttons, glows, borders, highlights, and interactive elements. Replace any gold/navy accent usage with this green. Glow effects on buttons, cards, and section accents should use a green radial or box-shadow glow consistent with the logo's energy style.

Use the uploaded photo (IMG_1534.jpeg) as the DJ Fizz professional portrait in the About section. Display it as a full, high-quality portrait image with a subtle green glow border or frame treatment consistent with the site's neon green aesthetic. Save the image as a static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/dj-fizz-portrait.jpg and reference it from the About section component.

Create a private Admin page at route /admin that displays all stored booking form submissions in a clean, readable table or card list. Each entry should show: name, email, phone, event type, event date, venue, guest count, additional details, and submission timestamp. Style the admin page using the site's black and neon green palette. No authentication is required for this initial version.

Fix the DJ Fizz portrait image in the About section so it displays correctly. The image is saved at frontend/public/assets/generated/dj-fizz-portrait.jpg. Update the element at the XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[1]/div[1]/div[2]/img[1] in the About component to reference the correct absolute path /assets/generated/dj-fizz-portrait.jpg (relative to the public root), ensuring the src attribute resolves properly in the deployed environment.

In the About section component (frontend/src/components/About.tsx), remove the six selected elements identified by the following XPaths: /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[1], /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2], /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[3], /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[1]/div[1], /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[1], and /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/div[3]/div[1]. These elements are the stat counter cards (and their inner content divs) in the About section. Delete the entire stat counter group container and all its children from the component JSX.

In the Hero component (frontend/src/components/Hero.tsx), update the element at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[1]/div[2]/div[1]/img[1] to reference a PNG version of the DJ Fizz logo. Generate a new PNG version of the existing logo image and save it as a static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/dj-fizz-logo.png, then update the img src attribute to /assets/generated/dj-fizz-logo.png.

In the Hero component (frontend/src/components/Hero.tsx), fix the DJ Fizz logo PNG image so the checkered transparent background pattern no longer shows. Ensure the image container and any parent wrapper elements have a background color of transparent or matching the site's dark base (#000000 / #0a0a0a), not a light or default browser background. Remove any background-color, bg-white, bg-gray, or similar light background classes from the logo's container element. The logo should render cleanly against the dark hero background with no visible checkerboard artifact.

Save the uploaded image (0E4EB4A7-858B-4D8C-9F78-10EFB5808419.png) as the new DJ Fizz logo static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/dj-fizz-logo.png, replacing the existing file. Then update the element at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[1]/div[2]/div[1]/img[1] in frontend/src/components/Hero.tsx to reference /assets/generated/dj-fizz-logo.png. Ensure the image container and any parent wrapper elements have no light background classes (no bg-white, bg-gray, or similar) so the logo renders cleanly against the dark hero background with no visible checkerboard artifact.

In the Hero component (frontend/src/components/Hero.tsx), add a solid black (#000000) background directly to the container element wrapping the element at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[1]/div[2]/div[1]/img[1]. Apply `bg-black` (or `style={{ backgroundColor: '#000000' }}`) to that immediate wrapper div so the checkered transparent pattern behind the logo PNG is completely hidden and the logo renders against a solid black background.

Save the uploaded image (BD9C94D8-6F5B-4ABB-9A7C-F15D1573FB8B.png) as a static asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/dj-fizz-hero-banner.png. Then update the element at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[1]/div[2]/div[1]/img[1] in frontend/src/components/Hero.tsx to reference /assets/generated/dj-fizz-hero-banner.png. The image must be displayed full-width across the entire hero section — remove any max-width, fixed-size, or logo-sized constraints on the image and its wrapper. Apply w-full, object-cover, and ensure the container spans 100% of the section width. Remove the solid black background from the image wrapper div since this banner image has a built-in dark background.

In the page's fourth section (section[4]), locate the fifth child container div (div[5]) inside the inner content wrapper at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[2]/div[2]/div[5]. Delete this entire div element and all its children from the component JSX, including the nested sub-element at /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[2]/div[2]/div[5]/div[2].

In the Gallery component (frontend/src/components/Gallery.tsx), delete the entire div element at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[2]/div[2]/div[5] and all its children, including the nested elements at /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[2]/div[2]/div[5]/div[2]/div[2]/p[1], /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[2]/div[2]/div[5]/div[2]/div[2]/h3[1], and /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[2]/div[2]/div[5]/div[2]/div[1]. Remove the complete fifth gallery card (div[5]) and all its descendant JSX from the component.

In the Gallery component (frontend/src/components/Gallery.tsx), delete the entire div element at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[2] and all its children, including the nested element at /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[2]/div[2]/div[5]/div[2]. This is the main content wrapper div of the Gallery section — remove it and all descendant JSX from the component.

In frontend/src/components/Gallery.tsx, completely remove any remaining content wrapper div and all its children from the Gallery section. The component should render only a minimal shell (e.g., the outer section element with an id for anchor linking) with no visible image cards, grid containers, headings nested inside content wrappers, or any other child content. Strip all JSX inside the section down to nothing — or at minimum remove every div, card, image, and text element that is currently rendering visible content inside the Gallery section on the page.

In frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx, remove the authentication check and any login gate that blocks access to the page. The /admin route must be fully accessible without any login or identity verification — remove any conditional rendering that shows an 'admin access required' or 'please log in' error message, and ensure all booking submissions are fetched and displayed immediately when the page loads without requiring authentication.

Fix the admin page (`frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx`) so that it correctly fetches and displays all stored booking submissions from the backend. Ensure the `useGetAllSubmissions` React Query hook in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` calls the correct backend query method, handles the returned data shape properly, and that `AdminPage.tsx` renders the submissions list without requiring authentication. Verify the actor is initialized in anonymous mode so the query can execute on page load without login.

Rewrite the `useGetAllSubmissions` hook in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` to call the correct backend query method (`getAllSubmissions`) using an anonymous actor (no authentication required). Ensure the hook correctly unpacks the returned array, handles both empty arrays and populated arrays, and does not depend on Internet Identity being logged in. The query should run automatically on page load with no manual trigger.

Update `frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx` to consume the fixed `useGetAllSubmissions` hook and render all returned submission records as styled cards. Each card must display: name, email, phone, event type, event date, venue, guest count, additional details, and submission timestamp. Remove any remaining authentication check or login gate. Show a clear empty-state message when no submissions exist. Show a loading indicator while the query is in flight. Show an error message if the query fails.

Ensure `frontend/src/components/BookingForm.tsx` uses the `useSubmitForm` mutation hook correctly and displays clear success and error feedback after submission. The mutation must call the backend `submitBooking` function with all 8 fields (name, email, phone, eventType, eventDate, venue, guestCount, additionalDetails) using an anonymous actor. On success, show a confirmation message and reset the form. On error, show an error message without losing the entered data.

In `frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx`, completely remove all authentication checks, login gates, and any conditional rendering that blocks or restricts access to the /admin route. The page must render immediately on load without requiring Internet Identity login or any identity verification. Remove all references to `useInternetIdentity`, `isAuthenticated`, or any auth state that gates the display of content. Ensure `useGetAllSubmissions` is called unconditionally on mount and submissions are displayed as soon as the query resolves.

new_feature_requests.summary: Cinematic hero section with headline 'Elevating Every Event.', subheadline, two CTA buttons, and animated stage lighting beams
About DJ Fizz section with professional portrait (uploaded photo), energetic bio copy, and signature-style name graphic
4-column services section (Weddings, Corporate Events, School Dances & Proms, Private Parties) with icons and Learn More buttons
Premium add-ons section with glowing card designs (Dance Floor Lighting, Uplighting, Ceremony Audio, Wireless Mics, Monogram Projection)
5 emotionally powerful testimonials covering professionalism, energy, sound clarity, communication, and crowd engagement
Grid-style event gallery with hover animations (wedding, corporate, school dance, DJ booth images)
Local SEO section with naturally integrated Raleigh, NC DJ keywords
Bold booking section with 8-field form, trust statement, and backend submission storage viewable on admin page
'More Than Just Music' section highlighting timeline management, crowd reading, and planner coordination
Admin page to view stored booking form submissions

Store booking form submissions in the Motoko backend. The backend actor must persist each submission with all 8 fields: name, email, phone, event type, event date, venue, estimated guest count, and additional details, along with a submission timestamp. Expose a query function to retrieve all stored submissions.
Replace the current DJ Fizz logo image in the Hero section with the newly uploaded PNG (0E4EB4A7-858B-4D8C-9F78-10EFB5808419.png), saving it as the new logo asset and updating the img src reference
Replace the current hero logo image with the newly uploaded DJ Fizz banner (DJ controller, headphones, neon green splatter) displayed as a full-width hero image
Delete the section at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/section[4]/div[2] (and nested element div[2]/div[2]/div[5]/div[2]) from the Gallery component
Fix booking form submission reliability (intermittent failures) and fix admin page not displaying stored submissions correctly

Audit and fix the Motoko backend `submitBooking` (or equivalent) update function to ensure it reliably persists every booking form submission without intermittent failures. Verify that the stable storage structure (e.g., array or map of submissions) is correctly initialized and that the function always appends the new entry and returns a success result. Add defensive handling so that any unexpected runtime error is caught and a meaningful error variant is returned rather than a silent failure.

Audit and rewrite the Motoko backend `submitBooking` function in `backend/main.mo` to guarantee reliable persistence of every submission. Ensure the stable `submissions` Map (or equivalent stable structure) is correctly declared with `stable` keyword, properly initialized, and that `submitBooking` always appends the new entry with an auto-incremented ID and returns `#ok`. Add a `getAllSubmissions` public query function that returns all stored submissions as an array of records including id, name, email, phone, eventType, eventDate, venue, guestCount, additionalDetails, and submittedAt timestamp. Eliminate any pattern that could cause silent failures or state loss across canister upgrades.

In `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts`, ensure the `useGetAllSubmissions` hook creates and uses a fully anonymous actor (no Internet Identity dependency) to call the `getAllSubmissions` backend query. The hook must not reference or depend on any auth state, must not wait for a logged-in identity, and must run the query automatically on mount. Correctly unpack the returned array of submission records.
